report_name: MITRE Corporation Hacked by Nation-State Actor Using Ivanti Zero-Days

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Nation-State Actor
threat_actors.description: A sophisticated nation-state actor that breached MITRE Corporation's network by exploiting two zero-day vulnerabilities in Ivanti Connect Secure appliances. The specific group was not publicly attributed by MITRE.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor exploited two zero-day vulnerabilities in public-facing Ivanti Connect Secure VPN appliances to gain initial access to MITRE's network.

attack_patterns.name: External Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: The attacker abused the Ivanti Connect Secure VPN service to gain initial access into the corporate network.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: After gaining an initial foothold, the attacker used compromised accounts to move laterally within MITRE's network.

attack_patterns.name: Modify Authentication Process: Multi-Factor Authentication
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor manipulated a compromised account's multi-factor authentication settings to bypass MFA controls and authenticate to other systems.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2023-46805
vulnerabilities.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability in Ivanti Connect Secure, likely one of the two zero-days exploited for initial access to MITRE's network.
vulnerabilities.kev_required_action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.
vulnerabilities.cisa_vuln_name: Ivanti Connect Secure and Policy Secure Authentication Bypass Vulnerability

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2024-21887
vulnerabilities.description: A command injection vulnerability in Ivanti Connect Secure, likely one of the two zero-days exploited for initial access to MITRE's network.
vulnerabilities.kev_required_action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.
vulnerabilities.cisa_vuln_name: Ivanti Connect Secure and Policy Secure Command Injection Vulnerability

identities.name: MITRE Corporation
identities.description: A U.S. government-funded research and development organization, creator of the ATT&CK framework, that was breached by a nation-state actor via Ivanti zero-day vulnerabilities.

infrastructure.name: Compromised Ivanti Connect Secure VPN
infrastructure.description: An Ivanti Connect Secure VPN appliance that was exploited by the threat actor to gain initial access and pivot into MITRE's internal network.

indicators.name: Anomalous MFA Configuration Change
indicators.pattern:
title: MFA Disabled for User Account
id: 2b1b8c7d-9e0f-4a1b-8c7d-9e0f4a1b8c7d
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects when multi-factor authentication is disabled for a user account. This could be indicative of an attacker with administrative access attempting to bypass MFA for a compromised account, as seen in the MITRE breach.
references:
  - https://www.cognisys.com/blog/the-biggest-cyber-attacks-and-vulnerabilities-of-april-2024/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2024-04-27
modified: 2024-04-27
tags:
  - attack.t1556.006
  - attack.defense_evasion
logsource:
  service: azure.audit
  category: iam
detection:
  selection:
    properties.activityDisplayName: 'Disable Strong Authentication'
    properties.result: 'success'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative action for user account recovery or troubleshooting. Should be investigated.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects when multi-factor authentication is disabled for a user account, which could be indicative of an attacker with administrative access attempting to bypass MFA for a compromised account, as seen in the MITRE breach.
